title: A Functional Architecture for Distributed Governance: Mechanism Design, Adversarial Validation, and Computational Evidence for Selective Institutional Distribution (Manuscript v1.13)
description: Working-paper MANUSCRIPT (bilingual: drafts/paper.md English, drafts/paper.es.md Spanish) proposing a functional architecture for distributed public-project governance: citizens allocate a mandate-recorded share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation. v1.13 (Path B) RETIRES the earlier compound value-per-budget multiplier as a calibrated effect: a pre-registered symmetric, selection-only stress test returned NO-GO (the distributed selection advantage is real but small — pooled ~0.026 of a full-information benchmark, below its 0.05 materiality gate). The paper's load-bearing contributions are the architecture and the qualitative mechanism direction (credit-pressured central selection mis-allocates relative to coverage-based distributed selection). This record is the MANUSCRIPT ONLY (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0); the full research corpus + code are a SEPARATE record (CC BY 4.0 + MIT; concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21193846). Controlling claim spec: research/claim-and-estimand-contract.md.
notes: MANUSCRIPT record (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0). Files to deposit: the rendered manuscript PDFs/HTML (EN + ES) and, if desired, docs/101 + the essay/explainer + reviewer packets (all CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 per LICENSE.md). Do NOT include the CC-BY corpus/code here — those go in the separate corpus record (.zenodo.json). Latest deposited manuscript before this: bundled inside the v1.12 corpus record (10.5281/zenodo.21252911), which was CC BY 4.0; the NC-ND manuscript separation begins with v1.13.
